ASSIGNED TO ARBITRATION FOR DEC-10-2012. NOV-14-2012 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. THIS COURT HAS NO JURISDICTION TO ENTERTAIN A CIVIL ACTION COLLATERALLY ATTACKING A CRIMINAL SENTENCE, LET ALONE A SENTENCE IMPOSED IN ANOTHER COUNTY. EVEN IF THE COURT COULD ADDRESS THE MERITS OF THE CASE, WHICH IT CANNOT, AND EVEN IF THERE WERE A CIVIL CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF A SENTENCING AGREEMENT, WHICH THERE IS NOT, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM WOULD FAIL ON THE MERITS FOR SEVERAL OTHER REASONS. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE, LET ALONE PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE OF, ANY ACT BY DEFENDANT THAT BREACHED THE AGREEMENT. RATHER, HE ALLEGES THAT ANOTHER ENTITY, WHICH IS NOT A PARTY TO THIS CASE, THE SAN MATEO DISTRICT ATTORNEY, BREACHED THE AGREEMENT. DEFENDANT PRODUCES SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS HAS RUN ON ANY PUTATIVE BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM, AS THE DATE OF THE ALLEGED BREACH OCCURRED ON SEPTEMBER 7, 2001, AND THIS ACTION WAS FILED IN FEBRUARY 2012. (DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE, EXHS. B, C; SEE ALSO CCP 337.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRODUCED ANY EVIDENCE SUPPORTING HIS ARGUMENT THAT THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PERIOD HAS BEEN TOLLED INDEFINITELY UNDER CCP 352. PLAINTIFF PRODUCES NO RELEVANT EVIDENCE THAT HE SUFFERED FROM POST TRAUMATIC STRESS DISORDER WHEN THE ALLEGED BREACH OCCURRED ON SEPTEMBER 7, 2001, OR THAT THE POST TRAUMATIC STRESS ORDER HAS RENDERED HIM INSANE AS CONTEMPLATED UNDER CCP 352. THE RECORD SHOWS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS AN ADEQUATE REMEDY AT LAW AVAILABLE TO CHALLENGE HIS SENTENCE THROUGH A WRIT OF A HABEAS CORPUS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ERIC WARNER, PLANTIFF IN PRO PER.
DUNCAN CARLING, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, FOR THE DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. This Court has no jurisdiction to entertain a civil action collaterally attacking a criminal sentence, let alone a sentence imposed in another county. Even if the Court could address the merits of the case, which it cannot, and even if there were a civil cause of action for breach of a sentencing agreement, which there is not, Plaintiff's claim would fail on the merits for several other reasons. Plaintiff does not allege, let alone present any evidence of, any act by Defendant that breached the agreement. Rather, he alleges that another entity, which is not a party to this case, the San Mateo District Attorney, breached the agreement. Defendant produces sufficient evidence that the statute of limitations has run on any putative breach of contract claim, as the date of the alleged breach occurred on September 7, 2001, and this action was filed in February 2012. (Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice, Exhs. B, C; See also CCP 337.) Plaintiff has not produced any evidence supporting his argument that the statute of limitations period has been tolled indefinitely under CCP 352. Plaintiff produces no relevant evidence that he suffered from Post Traumatic Stress Disorder when the alleged breach occurred on September 7, 2001, or that the Post Traumatic Stress order has rendered him insane as contemplated under CCP 352. The record shows that Plaintiff has an adequate remedy at law available to challenge his sentence through a writ of a habeas corpus. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-27-2013 Continued To Oct-08-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to October 8, 2013. The Court asks the City to provide immediately a courtesy copy of the opposition because the opposing party is incarcerated. The Court has the moving and reply papers. Please attach a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185. =(302/LCN)
